message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Arriola's Tortilleria (Indio)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~228 reviews; Google rating ~4.6 [exact Google count UNVERIFIED]; Tripadvisor and DoorDash also carry 100+ entries each
- Sentiment: mostly positive — consistently praised for fresh tortillas, salsa, tamales, chile verde/colorado, and Sunday menudo; 'community staple' framing dominates
- Longevity: Founded 1927 by Herlindo and Eulalia Arriola; at current Wilson Ave location since 1965; multi-generational family ownership (Ray Arriola, then grandson Daniel Hernandez; editorial coverage describes third-generation Becky Hernandez involvement). Roughly 99 years in business — among the oldest continuously operating Mexican food businesses in Southern California
- Credentials: ["California 56th Assembly District Small Business of the Year 2018 (Asm. Eduardo Garcia)","City of Indio recognition during National Hispanic Heritage Month (Oct 2015)","KCRW Good Food feature ('sold tortillas longer than anyone in Southern California')","LA Taco 'Where to Eat in Coachella & Indio' feature","Palm Springs Life feature ('Corn or Flour?') and Best Handmade Tamales listing","Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listing"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'oldest tortilleria Southern California', 'best tamales Coachella Valley / Palm Springs', and 'where to eat in Indio/Coachella' editorial lists (LA Taco, Palm Springs Life). Does NOT reliably surface for generic 'best Mexican restaurants Indio' or 'best Mexican Coachella Valley' Tripadvisor/Yelp top-10 listicles, where La Hacienda, Taqueria Guerrero, El Tranvia, and Jalisco tend to dominate. Essentially invisible to 'date night', 'fine dining', or corridor-anchor queries (correctly, since it is takeout-only)
- Site structured data: No verified first-party domain. The top Google hit 'arriolas-tortilleria.com-fnb.com' is a third-party SEO subdomain (com-fnb.com), not owner-controlled; similar shells exist on placejoys.com, wheree.com, weeblyte.com, postcard.inc, restaurantji, wanderlog. Real owner presence appears limited to a Facebook page and Instagram (@arriolas_tortilleria) plus Visit Greater Palm Springs and DoorDash/Order.online listings. No schema.org JSON-LD, no structured hours/menu under an owner-controlled domain — agents are reading aggregator scrape data, not first-party signals
- Visibility gap score: 8
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Southern California’s oldest tortilleria awaits","url":"https://www.kcrw.com/culture/shows/good-food/best-everyday-tortillas-california-desert-arriola-tortilleria"},{"title":"Arriola's Tortilleria, Indio - Menu, Reviews (171), Photos (49) - Restaurantji","url":"https://www.restaurantji.com/ca/indio/arriolas-tortilleria-/"},{"title":"Arriola's Tortilleria - Restaurant · Indio - Postcard.inc","url":"https://www.postcard.inc/places/arriolas-tortilleria-indio-BtCQHvgYoQG"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Arriola's Tortilleria in Indio:

## History and Founding

Founders Herlindo and Eulalia Arriola started making and selling tortillas out of their house around the corner in 1927. The family-owned business was established by Ray Arriola's parents, immigrants from Durango, Mexico, who began making and selling tortillas in 1927 out of their home.

## Modern Operations

They've been making fresh corn and flour tortillas out of its low-slung location since 1965, when a tortilla factory was built in 1965 on land adjacent to the original site.

## Oldest Tortilleria Claim

Arriola's Tortillas is the oldest continuously operating tortilleria in Southern California. The oldest, continuous operation by a decade belongs to the Arriolas, with comparisons to other venerable Southern California tortillerias such as Ramona's (1954) in South Central L.A., Carrillo's Tortilleria & Mexican Delicatessen (1943) in the San Fernando Valley, and Graciana Tortilla Factory (1937) in Sylmar.

## Family Ownership

The business is now in its fourth generation of ownership, with 30-year-old Ian Townley being the fourth generation of Arriola's.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Arriola's Tortilleria in Indio and its reviews:

## Location & Hours
Arriola's Tortilleria is located at 82721 Wilson Ave, Indio, CA 92201, and operates Monday-Saturday 8:00 AM-5:00 PM and Sunday 8:00 AM-12:00 PM.

## Overall Reception
Arriola's Tortilleria is a local gem serving delicious food that has become a beloved part of the community in Indio, with their nachos with meat and tamale boat being popular items, while the tamales and fresh tortillas are highly praised.

## Highly Praised Items
They are most famous for their chips and salsa, with almost every birthday, wedding, bbq, quincanera, and family event in town getting Arriola's chips. Reviewers rave about the carne asada burrito, tamales, and nachos with generous portions of meat.

## Key Features
All food is to-go with no seating. The restaurant is known for its flavorful burritos, including the Chile Verde and bean and cheese options, all at an affordable price of $10 or less.

## Historical Significance
They've been making fresh corn and flour tortillas out of its location since 1965, but founders Herlindo and Eulalia Arriola started making and selling tortillas out of their house around the corner in 1927.

## Top-Rated Mexican Restaurants in Indio:

The highest-rated Mexican restaurants in Indio include La Hacienda Mexican Restaurant, Pueblo Viejo Grill, Tacos Gonzalez, El Mexicali Cafe, and Arriola's Tortilleria.

Additional notable restaurants on Yelp's lists include:
- Outside The Masa, Flor De Jalisco, and El Mexicali Cafe II
- Huertas Restaurant, Guadalajara Cafe & Bar

## Highlights from Reviews:

La Hacienda Mexican Restaurant receives praise for having great food, good customer service, and good prices. 

Rincon Norteño Mexican Restaurant is a family-owned eatery that has been serving Mexican cuisine for over 50 years, known for its high level of service and top-notch cooking.

These rankings are current as of February-May 2026 based on the Yelp search results.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best places to find tamales in the Coachella Valley and Palm Springs area:

## Highly Recommended

Arriola's Tortilleria in Indio has been supplying tortillas to the valley for almost 100 years, and is recommended for traditional tamales. Their masa is firm but creamy, making the perfect casing for a traditional shredded pork filling.

## Other Notable Options

Top-rated tamale places in Palm Springs include Jenny's Tamales, Outside The Masa, Jalisco Restaurant, Villa Bakery, La Perlita, Felipe's, La Bonita's, Tac/Quila, and Cardenas Markets.

Asadero Los Corrales in Coachella is known for tamales, burritos, pozole, tortas, and more.

## Special Event

The Indio International Tamale Festival is held every December in Indio and is the largest festival of its kind in the world, attracting nearly 100,000 people every year with authentic, homemade tamales from all over Central and South America.

Based on the search results, here's what I found:

Democratic Assemblymember Eduardo Garcia recognized Arriola's Tortilleria as the 56th Assembly District's 2018 Small Business of the Year on Saturday, July 20 (the ceremony was held on July 21, 2018). 

The award recognized the restaurant's support of the community and steadfast commitment to customer service and high business ethics. Ray Arriola and his family were on hand for the event, along with Garcia and The Greater Coachella Chamber of Commerce with a formal Resolution from the California State Legislature celebrating the longstanding success of his business within the community.

Arriola's Tortilleria is a family-owned business that was established by Ray Arriola's parents, immigrants from Durango, Mexico, who began making and selling tortillas in 1927 out of their home. The tortilleria is located at 82721 Wilson Ave. in Indio.

I found several great options for date night restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Indio area with Mexican cuisine:

**Mexican Restaurants for Date Nights:**

Located at the stunning El Dorado Polo Club in Indio, this restaurant offers American and Mexican dishes enjoyed on an outdoor patio, with field-side outdoor seating and bar offering specialty cocktails.

A locally owned and operated spot off Jefferson Ave in Indio offers menu items such as trash burrito, enchiladas, and fried ice cream, with their Tamarindo Margarita and Soul-Strong Margarita being popular choices.

El Patio is a family-owned Mexican restaurant in nearby La Quinta that brings the flavors of Jalisco to Indio, renowned for its authentic cuisine and family recipes with traditional dishes such as Molcajete, served in a cooking stone.

A colorful neighborhood restaurant serves authentic Mexican fare and handcrafted cocktails, with its location noted in Desert Hot Springs.

Pueblo Viejo is a longtime valley favorite, authentic and full of flavor, where the vibe is relaxed, portions are generous, and the salsa is next level.

These restaurants offer a range of atmospheres from casual to more romantic settings, making them suitable options for date nights in the Coachella Valley/Indio area.

I found several relevant search results about Arriola Tortilleria in Indio with information about Becky Hernandez and Daniel Hernandez. Here's what I found:

## Key Information

Arriola's Tortilleria founders Herlindo and Eulalia Arriola started making and selling tortillas out of their house in 1927, and they've been making fresh corn and flour tortillas out of their current location since 1965.

**Family Leadership:**
Becky Hernandez, Ray Arriola's daughter, now runs the tortilleria. Ray Arriola is survived by his wife, Sophie Arriola, and children including Becky.

**Third and Fourth Generation:**
Ian Townley is the fourth generation of Arriola's. Daniel Hernandez is one of Ray Arriola's six grandchildren, making him part of the third generation. The photo caption mentions Ian Townley flanked by cousins Chris and Daniel Hernandez.

**Current Operations:**
The corporation is led by Daniel V Hernandez (Chief Executive Officer and Director), Erinn M Hernandez (Chief Financial Officer and Director), and Rebecca L Hernandez (Secretary), showing the family's continued involvement in running the business.

content.text: I have enough verification data. Submitting findings.

confirms: Yelp review count ~228 (confirmed by Yelp listing title: '130 Photos & 228 Reviews')
Founded 1927 by Herlindo and Eulalia Arriola — confirmed by KCRW Good Food and Ukenreport
Current Wilson Ave location since 1965 — confirmed by KCRW and Palm Springs Life
Multi-generational ownership: Ray Arriola, daughter Becky Hernandez now runs it; Daniel Hernandez (grandson) confirmed as third generation; Ian Townley fourth generation
Oldest continuously operating tortilleria in Southern California — explicitly stated by KCRW; older than Ramona's (1954), Carrillo's (1943), Graciana (1937)
California 56th Assembly District Small Business of the Year 2018 award from Asm. Eduardo Garcia (ceremony July 21, 2018) — confirmed by Ukenreport and coachellavalley.com
KCRW Good Food feature with the 'sold tortillas longer than anyone in Southern California' framing — confirmed verbatim in KCRW headline
LA Taco 'Where To Eat in Coachella & Indio' editorial inclusion — confirmed
Palm Springs Life 'Corn or Flour?' feature and Best Handmade Tamales listing — both confirmed
Visit Greater Palm Springs tourism listing — confirmed live at visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/arriolas-tortilleria/35538/
Surfaces strongly for 'oldest tortilleria Southern California' and 'best tamales Coachella Valley/Palm Springs' editorial queries

No first-party owner-controlled website — Visit Greater Palm Springs listing has no first-party URL; the com-fnb.com, placejoys.com, wheree.com, weeblyte.com results are third-party SEO shells, not owner domains
Owner presence limited to Facebook and Instagram (@arriolas_tortilleria) plus aggregators (DoorDash, Order.online, Visit GPS)
Does NOT dominate 'date night Coachella Valley' queries (Palm Springs Life date-night roundup surfaces other venues; Arriola's correctly absent since takeout-only)
contradicts: Claim says 'roughly 99 years in business' — as of 2026-06-09 it's actually 99 years complete (1927 to 2026 = 99); minor but the assessment's 'roughly 99' is fine. No contradiction worth flagging.

Claim lists top-10 Indio competitors as 'La Hacienda, Taqueria Guerrero, El Tranvia, and Jalisco' — Yelp's actual top-of-list for Indio surfaces La Hacienda, Pueblo Viejo Grill, Tacos Gonzalez, El Mexicali Cafe (and includes Arriola's itself). Taqueria Guerrero, El Tranvia, and Jalisco are NOT the Yelp top-of-stack for Indio specifically. The competitor set named in the claim is partially incorrect for the Indio Yelp listicle context.

Claim says Arriola's 'does NOT reliably surface' for generic 'best Mexican restaurants Indio' Yelp lists — actually Arriola's IS in Yelp's top-tier Indio Mexican results per the search summary (listed alongside La Hacienda, Pueblo Viejo, Tacos Gonzalez, El Mexicali). So this part of the visibility gap is overstated.
flagged_as_unverified: October 2015 City of Indio National Hispanic Heritage Month recognition — could not independently confirm in this pass (no direct source surfaced)
Exact Tripadvisor and DoorDash review counts of 100+ each — Tripadvisor and DoorDash listings exist (confirmed) but specific 100+ counts not independently verified in search snippets

Sentiment characterization (Sunday menudo, chile verde/colorado, 'community staple' framing) — directionally consistent with snippets praising tamales, chips/salsa, burritos, but specific menudo/chile colorado emphasis not directly confirmed in fetched snippets

Absence of schema.org JSON-LD on any owner-controlled domain — confirmed by absence of any owner domain; cannot verify a non-existent property, but the assertion is logically sound since no first-party site exists
input.notes: Core claims hold up well: founding year 1927, 1965 Wilson Ave location, multi-generational family ownership (Becky Hernandez running it now, Daniel Hernandez as third-gen, Ian Townley as fourth-gen — actually FOUR generations per KCRW, not just three as the claim implies), 'oldest tortilleria in Southern California' framing, 2018 Small Business of the Year, KCRW/LA Taco/Palm Springs Life features, no first-party domain. Two corrections worth noting: (1) Arriola's actually DOES appear in Yelp top-tier Indio Mexican results — the claim overstates its invisibility on generic listicles; (2) the named competitor set (Taqueria Guerrero, El Tranvia, Jalisco) doesn't match the actual Yelp Indio top-stack which features Pueblo Viejo, Tacos Gonzalez, El Mexicali alongside La Hacienda. Also worth flagging: lineage may be FOUR generations rather than three (Ian Townley is documented as fourth gen). Visibility gap score of 8 is defensible given total absence of first-party schema-bearing domain — agents are entirely reading aggregator scrape data.
